evals.name: create-from-brief
evals.prompt: I'm building a calm meditation web app. Create a DESIGN.md for my coding agent: soft sage greens, a warm off-white background, gentle rounded corners, and a friendly humanist sans-serif. Include a primary button. Save it as DESIGN.md.
evals.expected_output: A DESIGN.md file at the project root with valid YAML front matter (colors incl. a primary, typography, rounded, a button-primary component), canonical section order, and prose. Must lint with 0 errors.

assertions.text: A file named DESIGN.md was produced
Front matter contains a 'colors' map with a 'primary' key
Defines at least one typography token and a components entry
Running scripts/dmd.py lint on it yields 0 errors
Markdown ## sections appear in canonical order (Overview before Colors before Typography ...)

evals.name: validate-broken-ref
evals.prompt: Can you check whether my design tokens file is valid? It's the attached DESIGN.md — something feels off with the button.
evals.expected_output: Identifies the broken token reference {colors.brand} as an error (broken-ref), reports it clearly, and ideally suggests the fix (the color is named 'accent'). Should run the linter rather than eyeballing.

assertions.text: Reports that the file has a validation error (non-zero errors)
Points to the button-primary component as the location

evals.name: live-preview
evals.prompt: Generate a visual live preview of this design system so I can see the palette and type scale in my browser. File is attached.
evals.expected_output: A self-contained .html preview file (color swatches, type scale, components, spacing/radius) generated from the DESIGN.md, shared with the user. No remote assets.

assertions.text: The HTML renders color swatches for the palette
The HTML contains no remote <script> or external asset references
The preview is shared/offered to the user to open
